Class GlobalExceptionHandler

java.lang.Object
com.bank.core.infrastructure.in.web.GlobalExceptionHandler
@RestControllerAdvice public class GlobalExceptionHandler extends Object
Global exception handler that maps domain and infrastructure exceptions to standardised HTTP error responses.

All error responses share the ApiErrorResponse envelope, ensuring API consumers can parse failures uniformly. Exception-to-status mappings:

  • AccountNotFoundException → 404 Not Found
  • InsufficientFundsException → 422 Unprocessable Entity
  • AccountBlockedException → 422 Unprocessable Entity
  • DuplicateTransactionException → 409 Conflict
  • OptimisticLockingFailureException → 409 Conflict
  • MethodArgumentNotValidException → 400 Bad Request (with field errors)
  • Exception (catch-all) → 500 Internal Server Error
Since:
1.0.0
Version:
1.0.0
Author:
Core Banking Team

    private ApiErrorResponse
    buildError(HttpStatus status, String message, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest request)
    Builds a standard ApiErrorResponse with an empty field-error list.
    ApiErrorResponse
    handleAccountBlocked(AccountBlockedException ex, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest request)
    Handles AccountBlockedException (HTTP 422).
    ApiErrorResponse
    handleAccountNotFound(AccountNotFoundException ex, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest request)
    Handles AccountNotFoundException (HTTP 404).
    ApiErrorResponse
    handleDuplicateTransaction(DuplicateTransactionException ex, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest request)
    Handles DuplicateTransactionException (HTTP 409).
    ApiErrorResponse

    Catch-all handler for unexpected exceptions (HTTP 500).
    ApiErrorResponse
    handleValidation(MethodArgumentNotValidException ex, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest request)
    Handles MethodArgumentNotValidException (HTTP 400).

    • GlobalExceptionHandler

      public GlobalExceptionHandler()

  • Method Details

    • handleAccountNotFound

      @ExceptionHandler(AccountNotFoundException.class) @ResponseStatus(NOT_FOUND) public ApiErrorResponse handleAccountNotFound(AccountNotFoundException ex, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest request)
      Handles AccountNotFoundException (HTTP 404).
      Parameters:
      ex - the exception
      request - the originating HTTP request
      Returns:
      a standardised error response

    • handleInsufficientFunds

      @ExceptionHandler(InsufficientFundsException.class) @ResponseStatus(UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY) public ApiErrorResponse handleInsufficientFunds(InsufficientFundsException ex, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest request)
      Handles InsufficientFundsException (HTTP 422).
      Parameters:
      ex - the exception
      request - the originating HTTP request
      Returns:
      a standardised error response

    • handleAccountBlocked

      @ExceptionHandler(AccountBlockedException.class) @ResponseStatus(UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY) public ApiErrorResponse handleAccountBlocked(AccountBlockedException ex, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest request)
      Handles AccountBlockedException (HTTP 422).
      Parameters:
      ex - the exception
      request - the originating HTTP request
      Returns:
      a standardised error response

    • handleDuplicateTransaction

      @ExceptionHandler(DuplicateTransactionException.class) @ResponseStatus(CONFLICT) public ApiErrorResponse handleDuplicateTransaction(DuplicateTransactionException ex, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest request)
      Handles DuplicateTransactionException (HTTP 409).
      Parameters:
      ex - the exception
      request - the originating HTTP request
      Returns:
      a standardised error response

    • handleOptimisticLock

      @ExceptionHandler(OptimisticLockingFailureException.class) @ResponseStatus(CONFLICT) public ApiErrorResponse handleOptimisticLock(OptimisticLockingFailureException ex, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest request)
      Handles OptimisticLockingFailureException (HTTP 409).

      This is thrown when two concurrent transfer requests target the same account and one of them loses the optimistic lock race. The losing request should be retried by the client.

      Parameters:
      ex - the exception
      request - the originating HTTP request
      Returns:
      a standardised error response

    • handleValidation

      @ExceptionHandler(MethodArgumentNotValidException.class) @ResponseStatus(BAD_REQUEST) public ApiErrorResponse handleValidation(MethodArgumentNotValidException ex, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest request)
      Handles MethodArgumentNotValidException (HTTP 400).

      Returns a list of field-level validation errors in the errors field of the response envelope.

      Parameters:
      ex - the exception
      request - the originating HTTP request
      Returns:
      a standardised error response with field-level details

    • handleUnexpected

      @ExceptionHandler(Exception.class) @ResponseStatus(INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR) public ApiErrorResponse handleUnexpected(Exception ex, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest request)
      Catch-all handler for unexpected exceptions (HTTP 500).
      Parameters:
      ex - the exception
      request - the originating HTTP request
      Returns:
      a standardised error response

    • buildError

      private ApiErrorResponse buildError(HttpStatus status, String message, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest request)
      Builds a standard ApiErrorResponse with an empty field-error list.
      Parameters:
      status - the HTTP status to encode in the response body
      message - the developer-friendly error message
      request - the originating HTTP request
      Returns:
      the assembled error response
